ground_truth.title: 20 Best Cloud Management Platforms In 2026
ground_truth.author: nOps
ground_truth.main_content:
Reducing cloud waste was named this year's top challenge by the FinOps foundation. Yet, organizations are facing challenges like lack of visibility, complex pricing models, and inadequate tooling making it difficult to take action on cloud optimization. Luckily, the latest and greatest automation tools can help. We put together this essential guide to the best Cloud Management Platforms of 2025, with rankings, pros and cons, updated pricing information, and more.

Cloud Management Platforms (CMP) help businesses oversee and control their cloud infrastructure. They provide a centralized interface to manage resources, track usage, and enforce policies across cloud environments. CMPs are especially useful for organizations using multiple clouds or complex deployments. Their main goal is to improve efficiency, visibility, and cost control.

Key Finance Use Cases for CMPs include tracking and optimizing cloud resource costs, budgeting and enforcing spending limits, enabling predictive analytics for cloud spending trends, and facilitating detailed billing and cost allocation across departments.

Key Engineering Use Cases include visibility into relevant costs, efficiently accessing and provisioning cloud resources, automating cloud management tasks, maintaining performance with autoscaling, and promoting real-time cloud compliance and governance.

Key Leadership Use Cases include gaining a unified view of cloud performance and spending, making data-driven decisions regarding cloud strategy, ensuring alignment between IT operations and business objectives, and demonstrating ROI on cloud initiatives.

The article then provides detailed reviews of 20 cloud management platforms: nOps, IBM Cloud Orchestrator, Apache CloudStack, Apptio Cloudability, Morpheus Data, CloudHealth by VMware, Terraform, OpenStack, CloudBolt, CloudCheckr, Flexera, Datadog, Rubrik, and Nutanix, each with feature highlights, pricing information, target use cases, and ratings.
with: Reducing cloud waste was named this year's top challenge by the FinOps foundation.
Cloud Management Platforms (CMP) help businesses oversee and control their cloud infrastructure.
CMPs provide a centralized interface to manage resources, track usage, and enforce policies across cloud environments.
nOps is an all-in-one AWS cost optimization platform that helps users reduce their costs by up to 60% on autopilot.
Terraform is an open-source infrastructure as code (IaC) tool developed by HashiCorp.
